title: Derris muehlbergiana
intro: Derris muehlbergiana, also known as the Brazilian rotenone tree, is a leguminous plant native to tropical regions of Central and South America. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Derris muehlbergiana include rotenone, a potent insecticide and natural insect repellent, as well as other alkaloids and flavonoids with potential medicinal value. In herbal medicine, Derris muehlbergiana is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, or used in the form of a tincture or ointment to treat skin conditions and wounds.
taxonomy: Derris muehlbergiana is classified in the family Leguminosae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as legumes. The genus Derris is further divided into several species, with Derris muehlbergiana being one of them. This plant is native to tropical regions and is known for its toxic properties. Derris muehlbergiana is used in traditional medicine and as a source of rotenone, a natural insecticide. It is also closely related to other Derris species, such as Derris elliptica.
uses: Derris muehlbergiana has been used in traditional medicine due to its purported anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Extracts from the plant have been reported to exhibit antioxidant activity, which may contribute to its potential health benefits. The plant is also thought to have antiparasitic properties, making it a potential natural remedy for certain parasitic infections. Additionally, Derris muehlbergiana may possess analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which could make it useful for managing pain and inflammation. However, further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal uses and potential applications.

parts: Derris muehlbergiana, also known as the Japanese rotenone tree, is primarily valued for its roots and seeds. side_effects: Derris muehlbergiana is known to be toxic and can cause various side effects if ingested or if its sap comes into contact with the skin. Its sap can cause severe skin irritation, including blisters and burns, upon contact. Ingestion of the plant can lead to symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. The toxic compounds in Derris muehlbergiana can also cause neurological symptoms like headaches and dizziness. Prolonged exposure to the plant's toxins can result in more severe health issues, including organ damage and even death.
